item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1'd mostly for mixing two pieces of Foundation lore that should honestly be completely incompatible.

I honestly don't like the SPC very much (I feel similarly about most jokey-on-mainlist things), but the article is well-written and I think you pulled off what you were going for, so up it goes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry you didn't find it funny enough, humor is hard as it is rather subjective. Going that route while having the underlying screamer theme of criticism probably required a larger sampling of eyes in the drafting phase to see if it is hitting at least something for the largest audience possible.

To be honest, I also just wanted an excuse to give the center attack dolphins.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The cardinal sin of any article intended to criticize the reader for laughing along is not actually being funny. This is, coincidentally, also a major faux pas when writing the center, unless you decide to get really weird with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. It takes an inherently silly premise of Pattern Screamers and SPC coming together to punch the shit out of some sharks and makes it work on an entirely unironic level. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How can this not have more upvotes? Here, take my supply, you deserve it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
EDIT — September 8: In hindsight, I was being too hard on this article. There's nothing wrong here aside from my few quibbles about the use of the Shark Punching Centre. In fact, there's some great content here! This Centre-Pattern Screamer collaboration is very unique. So, take my following thoughts with a grain of salt.

I will admit that the only thing holding me back from upvoting is the Centre's placement into the mainline SCP universe, but with neither an in-universe nor out-of-universe justification for that.

Thoughts
– hide block

Forgive me if this feedback becomes too heavy-handed.

So that I don't bury the lede: I enjoyed the "weaponized pattern screamer whalesong" concept. This team's writing achieves hilarious shenanigans in a proper clinical tone. But one thing the article flat-out ignores is that the SPC is almost exclusively, and rather explicitly, an alt-universe Group of Interest. Apart from "they punch sharks", their alt-universe nature and the unique identity that stems from it is the only thing that keeps them cohesive and consistent. And I think it's a mistake to ignore that.

Shark Punching Center cameos used to be a throwaway joke. The premise was simple: sharks exist, and this bizarro Foundation is devoting their entire existence to punching them. And it got written like that for a while because it was funny. But over time — frankly? The joke got stale.

A few years ago, the GOI took a sharp turn into "alt-universe" territory. The SPC was transformed from "silly people doing silly things" into "serious people being forced to deal with a silly world" — where everyone is one punch away from completely losing their shit at the absurdity of their existence. And the concept benefitted immensely from that evolution. When I think of SPC, that's precisely what I imagine, and I think that transformation was when the SPC became a fresh new thing with its own identity.

I'll admit — it's silly that someone would so fervently argue against "there is no canon" for something so ridiculous as the SPC. Maybe I'm just being pedantic for opposing this. And I'm not even fully against writing an interaction between the Foundation and the Centre; hell, last year, I helped write exactly that, with not_a_seagull (as above) and a couple of other writers.

TLDR: I guess what I'm trying to say here is. I dislike that the SPC's depiction here is inconsistent with other modern SPC articles. Don't get me wrong, this article is decently written, and it emulates classic SPC shenanigans and professional Foundation tone well on their own separate merits. But it would have a much better grip on its concepts if it relied on the stronger core a contemporary SPC has to offer. I hope this makes sense; no vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of course it's canon. Me and Nameless Mediocre just wrote it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Technically, interaction between SPC and the Foundation is canon. Look at this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good and funny article, but you are missing a space between "SCP-4373" and "becomes" in the second sentence of the fifth paragraph.

Edit: mistake fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a good and funny article. Though I imagined SPC to be in a different universe due to their extreme obsession with sharks but different interpretations.

item.title: Re: Spoiler and image attribution post
item.content:encoded: Thanks for thanking the thanker for the thanks. Also thanks for the thanks.

item.title: Re: Spoiler and image attribution post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for making the thanks.

Special thanks to FloppyPhoenix for giving some advice, DrChandra for giving advice for some of the dialouge and AlanDaris for taking a look.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I took the liberty of correcting the stray "XXXXs" in your image captions. Highly amusing URLs, too.

item.title: Spoiler and image attribution post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to T Rutherford for crit along with those mentioned by Blue below!

+ SPOILERS
- Close

You may want to look at the source code. You may have also heard some odd distortion sounds in the audio files. These are from something called a spectrogram, where we hide images inside audio files. Below are links to the image hidden inside each of the three audio files but you can view them yourselves in the audio if you have something like the program Audacity.

http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/local--files/collab:nameless-mediocre-and-thebluehour/Spectrogram%201.PNG

http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/local--files/collab:nameless-mediocre-and-thebluehour/Spectrogram%202.PNG

http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/local--files/collab:nameless-mediocre-and-thebluehour/Spectrogram%203.PNG

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
UPD because I couldn't articulate my thoughts well enough.

While the concept is fun and there are lots of quality dialogue, especially on SPC's part, (I really enjoyed that "we taught pattern screamers to punch sharks"), I still think that some parts of the article are a little excessive and having both SCP and SPC existing in the same reality is a bit of a stretch, as I told you at the draft stage.

Overall, I've looked through this article again and decided to switch to novote. Hopefully, it's fine with you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the first half of my and Nameless Mediocre's entry for the collaberation contest. As you can tell just by reading this, it's a collab between the Shark Punching Center and the Pattern Screamers.

I had the idea of using SCP X Pattern Screamer and Nameless Mediocre came up with the idea for the SCP itself (my origional idea had been a more direct reference to SCP-3930).

This was really a collaberation in every sense of the word. Nameless Mediocre did some last minute maintenance, got the audio logs and made the O5 email. I wrote the interview and the SPC email. The Description and discovery parts were a joint effort between us.

+ show block
– hide block
